row_level_output.notes: Fine-grained Semantic Entropy bins are analysis metadata only and never replace the row-level operational label field.

candidate_pair_policy_summary.candidate_policy: Each prompt contributes one dataset-provided correct candidate and one dataset-provided incorrect or hallucinated candidate. Candidates are fixed before model scoring; no answer is generated for later correctness labeling.

selection.correct_candidate: Select exactly one deterministic correct candidate from dataset-provided correct_answers[] per prompt.
selection.incorrect_candidate: Select exactly one deterministic incorrect/hallucinated candidate from dataset-provided incorrect_answers[] per prompt.
selection.determinism: Use the configured dataset seed and stable prompt identity so the same prompt always yields the same correct and incorrect candidate.

label_source.details: TruthfulQA dataset-provided correct_answers[] and incorrect_answers[] create one deterministic correct and one deterministic incorrect/hallucinated candidate per prompt.

selection.determinism: No synthetic candidate generation; source fields define the fixed pair.

label_source.details: HaluEval-QA dataset-provided right_answer and hallucinated_answer are used directly as the fixed correct and hallucinated candidates.

datasets.name: TruthfulQA

label_policy.correctness_judgment: Dataset-provided candidate role decides correctness: selected correct_answers[] candidate is correct; selected incorrect_answers[] candidate is incorrect/hallucinated.
label_policy.operational_label_assignment: Archived diagnostic artifacts only: correct -> NORMAL; incorrect/hallucinated candidates apply the fixed registry Semantic Entropy thresholds for HIGH_DIVERSITY, LOW_DIVERSITY, or AMBIGUOUS_INCORRECT. Thesis-valid analyses use annotation-backed correctness plus corpus-axis bins.
label_policy.row_level_output: Archived diagnostic feature rows include a label field with one explicit operational TypeLabel value; thesis-valid rows must keep annotation-backed correctness as the primary label.

selection.correct_candidate: Select exactly one deterministic correct candidate from dataset-provided correct_answers[] per prompt.
selection.incorrect_candidate: Select exactly one deterministic incorrect/hallucinated candidate from dataset-provided incorrect_answers[] per prompt.
selection.determinism: Use the configured dataset seed and stable prompt identity so the same prompt always yields the same correct and incorrect candidate.

datasets.notes: Mandatory active paired dataset. Loader must build fixed candidate rows from dataset-provided correct_answers[] and incorrect_answers[] without generated-answer correctness labeling.

datasets.name: HaluEval-QA

label_policy.correctness_judgment: Dataset-provided candidate role decides correctness: right_answer is correct; hallucinated_answer is incorrect/hallucinated.
label_policy.operational_label_assignment: Archived diagnostic artifacts only: correct -> NORMAL; incorrect/hallucinated candidates apply the fixed registry Semantic Entropy thresholds for HIGH_DIVERSITY, LOW_DIVERSITY, or AMBIGUOUS_INCORRECT. Thesis-valid analyses use annotation-backed correctness plus corpus-axis bins.
label_policy.row_level_output: Archived diagnostic feature rows include a label field with one explicit operational TypeLabel value; thesis-valid rows must keep annotation-backed correctness as the primary label.

selection.determinism: No synthetic candidate generation; source fields define the fixed pair.

datasets.notes: Mandatory active paired dataset. Loader must use right_answer and hallucinated_answer directly without generated-answer correctness labeling.
